title: DECISION: No silent degradation — VectorStore-only search path, SemanticIndex fallback removed
description:
## Decision

VectorStore is the ONLY search path. The SemanticIndex fallback and JSON cache deserialization (load_with_lock, 1.1GB HashMap) are removed from the query flow entirely. On any VectorStore failure (load/build/search/save), propagate a detailed error message to the user — never silently degrade to the slow path.

## Rationale

Silent fallback to SemanticIndex (4-5s cold JSON load + per-query ONNX model reload) makes the user believe that IS the tool's performance. They never discover the fast path failed, never report the bug, and never get it fixed. The only acceptable fallback is a clear, actionable error message that tells the user what broke and how to fix it.

A tool should run at 100% performance or tell you why it can't. Running in degraded mode without the user's knowledge is a UX anti-pattern — it hides bugs behind mediocre-but-functional behavior.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Keep SemanticIndex fallback (current design)**: Rejected — hides VectorStore failures from users, makes performance bugs invisible, and preserves the slow JSON cache load path that m01/ac0 exist to eliminate.
- **Fallback with a visible warning**: Rejected — warnings are ignored by users, especially in CLI tool output. The tool still "works" so the warning is treated as noise.
- **Fallback with opt-in flag (--allow-degraded)**: Rejected — over-engineered for the problem. If VectorStore is broken, fix VectorStore.

## Affects

- TLDR-zxb: Wire search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP (must implement error-only, no fallback)
- TLDR-m01: Rewire EmbeddingCache onto usearch Index (store_search.rs fallback code removed)
- store_search.rs: SemanticIndex import and fallback branch removed entirely
close_reason: Decision recorded and linked to zxb + m01. Enforced in zxb implementation.

title: DECISION: Incremental re-indexing lives in the daemon (RAM writable index), gated on warm path
description: Because view() is read-only (see storage-split decision), incremental updates (remove stale function vectors + add changed ones, instead of the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable usearch Index. DECISION: that index lives in the DAEMON (TLDR-atc). The daemon holds the writable Index, watches file mtimes, applies per-function deltas, and periodically save()s to disk. Cold CLI invocations use read-only view() of the last saved file. CONSEQUENCE: incremental indexing is NOT a property of usearch alone — it is unlocked by usearch + the daemon warm path together. This elevates TLDR-atc from a latency win to the keystone for incremental indexing.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Add daemon warm path for semantic search (no semantic route exists — every query is cold)
description: DISCOVERED 2026-06-01 via daemon route scan + tldr explain. tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes. crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. CONSEQUENCE: every 'tldr semantic'/'similar'/'embed' invocation runs COLD in a fresh process -> SemanticIndex::build -> EmbeddingCache::open -> load_with_lock (cache.rs:236) deserializes the full ~1.1GB cache each time (RSS ~8GB), reloads the 110MB ONNX model (~0.5s), then embeds the query (~0.2s). For warm/interactive use this is a BIGGER lever than usearch: usearch view() removes the 4.3s load, but a daemon warm path removes the load AND the 0.5s model load AND keeps the index resident -> ac0 estimated ~230ms warm. SCOPE: add /semantic (+/similar,/embed) daemon route + handler that holds a warm SemanticIndex (mmap'd vectors once usearch lands) + a warm Embedder (model loaded once) in DaemonState, keyed by project+model+mtimes like other warm caches. Pairs with TLDR-ac0 (usearch storage) and TLDR-h27/y0q (CLI correctness).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic/similar/embed route through the daemon when running; warm query latency ~200-300ms (no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload); cold fallback preserved when daemon absent; results identical to cold path.
notes:
UNLOCKS INCREMENTAL INDEXING (research 2026-06-01): usearch view()'d mmap index is READ-ONLY (issue#97 — cannot add to disk). Incremental updates (remove stale keys + add changed-function vectors, avoiding the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable index. The daemon warm path is exactly that home: hold the writable usearch Index in DaemonState, apply deltas on file-mtime change, periodically save(). So this P0 is not just a latency win — it's the prerequisite for incremental re-indexing. Cold CLI keeps using read-only view().
2026-06-02 STATUS: warm path + root-relative cache keys committed (401a7ac); off-executor spawn_blocking committed (7199f03). REMAINING: live stop-mid-build validation (was pending the cache being free). The spawn_blocking follow-up bug (handle_notify on async thread) is TLDR-qr9, now folded into TLDR-ac0.2 (in-daemon watcher → internal channel). Consider closing atc once the live stop-mid-build check is done.
close_reason: Warm path ships VectorStore — load_or_build_store is near-instant on fresh store. Daemon holds resident (Model, VectorStore). Remaining improvements (incremental delta, watcher, concurrent reads) are separate issues (t8f, ac0.2, ac0.1).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: POST-ZXB (2026-06-02): The daemon now holds a resident VectorStore (Mutex<Option<(Model, VectorStore)>>) instead of SemanticIndex. Warm command uses load_or_build_store — near-instant when store is fresh on disk. The original atc acceptance ("warm query latency ~200-300ms, no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload") is MET for the common case (store exists, nothing changed). The "cold fallback preserved when daemon absent" criterion changes per TLDR-lx7: no fallback, errors propagate. Consider closing atc — the warm path works, remaining improvements (incremental delta, watcher) are separate issues (t8f, ac0.2).

title: Default 0.50 similarity threshold miscalibrated for query-prefix regime (hides correct results)
description: DISCOVERED while dogfooding tldr semantic on our own codebase (TLDR-blm work). The Arctic query prefix (TLDR-dlk, default-ON) improves RANKING decisively (eval recall@5 0.071->0.857) but LOWERS absolute cosine scores, because the asymmetric prefix puts query embeddings in a different region than raw-code passages (query.passage cosine < passage.passage cosine). The user-facing 'tldr semantic' applies a fixed default --threshold 0.50. Result: the CORRECT answer for 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' is cosine_similarity() but it scores 0.46 -> filtered out -> CLI prints 'No matches found above threshold'. So the feature looks BROKEN to a user despite the recall win. The eval harness bypasses the threshold (pure ranking) which is why it reported 0.857. FIX OPTIONS: (a) lower/remove the default threshold and rely on top-k ranking; (b) recalibrate threshold for the prefixed-query score distribution; (c) make threshold prefix-aware. Repro: tldr semantic 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' crates/tldr-core/src/semantic --top 3  (default threshold -> no matches; --threshold 0.0 -> cosine_similarity appears at rank 3, score 0.46).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic with default settings surfaces the correct top-ranked result for the gold queries (not filtered by threshold); eval-measured ranking and CLI-visible results agree.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm epic. Surfaced by the prefix verdict; a user-facing gap between eval-measured ranking (good) and CLI-visible results (threshold-suppressed).
close_reason: Default semantic threshold 0.5->0.0 (rank top-N, no cutoff) so the prefix-regime scores (~0.46) are no longer hidden; also fixed the formatter's hardcoded 0.50 display to show the actual threshold. PROVEN: 'tldr semantic <q>' with default settings now returns cosine_similarity instead of 'No matches'. semantic.rs.

title: zxb.4: Wire daemon warm+query onto resident VectorStore
description: Replace Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>> with Mutex<Option<VectorStore>> in DaemonState. Warm command: VectorStore::load (or build+save on miss) + corpus digest check. Query: embed query + search the resident store. Freshness check at load/notify time, NOT per query. Factor the search core from zxb.1 is reused here against the resident store. This is the biggest beneficiary — 4.3s JSON load on every warm becomes near-instant on the common (nothing changed) case.
close_reason: Done. Daemon semantic_index (Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>) replaced with semantic_store (Mutex<Option<(EmbeddingModel, VectorStore)>>). Warm uses load_or_build_store (near-instant on fresh store). Query uses query_store against resident store. Notify clears the slot. Build clean.

title: zxb.3: Wire embed command (embed.rs) onto VectorStore::build+save
description: Replace the manual chunk+EmbeddingCache+Embedder flow in embed.rs with VectorStore::build()+save(). The embed command becomes: build the store, persist it, report stats. EmbeddingCache stays as the build-time dedup layer inside VectorStore::build (not removed — that is TLDR-3gj).
close_reason: Done. embed.rs rewired to load_or_build_store(). Removed manual chunk/cache/embedder flow, --include-vectors flag (vectors in store). Build clean.

title: zxb.2: Wire CLI cold search path (semantic.rs) onto search_with_store
description: Replace SemanticIndex::build().search() at semantic.rs:153 with search_with_store(). Decide store_dir location (global cache sibling). Add store-outside-corpus guard. Dense-only path (non-hybrid). Hybrid --hybrid flag keeps SemanticIndex for now (see zxb.5).
close_reason: Done. CLI dense-only path wired to search_with_store(). store_dir_for() added to tldr-core for shared daemon/CLI path resolution. Hybrid stays on SemanticIndex (zxb.5). Build clean, no warnings.

title: zxb.1: Strip SemanticIndex fallback from store_search.rs (TLDR-lx7)
description: Foundation for all other zxb sub-tasks. Remove the SemanticIndex fallback branch from search_with_store(). On any VectorStore failure (load/build/search), propagate a detailed error message — never silently degrade. Changes: (1) Remove SemanticIndex import from store_search.rs. (2) Remove the Err(e) => { eprintln warn; SemanticIndex::build fallback } branch. (3) VectorStore errors propagate as TldrResult::Err with actionable messages. (4) Factor out the search core (embed query + search store + hits_to_report) so the daemon can reuse it against a resident store without the load/build wrapper. (5) Update tests. Does NOT touch any call site — just the helper module.
close_reason: Done. SemanticIndex fallback removed from store_search.rs. Three public functions: search_with_store (cold CLI), load_or_build_store (daemon warm), query_store (daemon per-query). All errors propagate. 7/7 unit tests pass, build clean.

title: DECISION: Route 2 — filesystem watching lives in the Rust daemon (notify crate); C++ supervisor reduced to launchd lifecycle
description: Chosen over keeping the external C++ supervisor (tldr-cli-demon) as the watcher driving the daemon via the IPC Notify command. The in-daemon notify-crate watcher (TLDR-ac0.2) is co-located with the in-RAM index it mutates: event -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker, no cross-process hop. Rationale: consolidation (one Rust process), dissolves TLDR-qr9 by construction, and makes incremental delta (t8f) an in-process channel not an IPC contract. Explicitly NOT a performance claim (notify and the C++ fsnotifier use the same OS primitives). The C++ supervisor shrinks to a launchd-backed babysitter or is retired (TLDR-ejm). HARD precondition: store/cache dir MUST be outside the watched corpus (self-write loop). User decision, 2026-06-02.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-02). Durable decision; closing so it stays queryable without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if revisited.

title: DECISION: Daemon vector index is fully in-memory (RwLock-resident), not view()/mmap
description: The daemon holds the vector index FULLY IN RAM (load() into a writable Index behind the IndexManager RwLock, mutated in place), NOT served via usearch view()/mmap. Refines TLDR-zl8 (view-for-queries / load-for-mutation) into a two-path model: daemon/warm path = RAM-resident RwLock<VectorStore> with concurrent reads + in-place delta writes; cold CLI (no daemon) MAY view()/mmap or also load to RAM. CONSEQUENCE: the view()/mmap GC reader-grace race (TLDR-pdb) no longer governs daemon query+reindex safety (the RwLock does); pdb narrows to cold-CLI-only and may be moot. Compiler-proven prerequisite (memory concurrency-sync-evidence-2026-06-02): VectorStore + Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> are Send+Sync with zero surgery. User decision, 2026-06-02.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-02). Durable decision; closing so it stays queryable without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if revisited.

title: In-daemon filesystem watcher (notify crate) — replace IPC Notify + C++ fsnotifier; rewrite handle_notify to channel
description: Bring file-watch detection INTO the Rust daemon via the notify crate (+ notify-debouncer-full), co-located with the in-RAM index it mutates. Watcher thread -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker. Removes the cross-process C++ fsnotifier->IPC hop and dissolves TLDR-qr9 by construction (invalidation flows via channel, never an async-thread mutex). The Notify IPC command becomes optional (keep as manual git-hook poke or delete). Rewrites the stubbed handle_notify (daemon.rs:1172 — currently nukes the whole index; comment admits 'In full implementation, would spawn background reindex task').
design: Route 2 (chosen 2026-06-02 over keeping C++ fsnotifier+IPC). Watch the canonical project root recursively on daemon start; stop on shutdown. Reuse chunker::enumerate_corpus_files as the SINGLE membership filter (watch-set == index-set, coupling-proof per kkt). HARD PRECONDITION: store_dir + cache_dir MUST be outside the watched corpus, else the daemon's own index writes fire events -> reindex -> write -> infinite loop (even more critical with a live watcher than with the poll gate). Debounce editor save-storms / git-checkout bursts via notify-debouncer-full. Honest framing: notify is NOT faster than fsnotifier (same OS primitives FSEvents/kqueue); the win is consolidation (one Rust process), killing qr9, and making t8f an in-process channel not an IPC contract — NOT performance.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon self-watches via notify; file create/modify/delete -> dirty events on internal channel (no IPC); a write INSIDE the store dir triggers nothing (self-write-loop trap test); qr9 invalidation no longer touches a lock on the async path.
notes:
2026-06-02 (Codex review): (1) CORRECT WORDING — handle_notify (daemon.rs:1172) is NOT a full stub: it already records dirty files, invalidates the salsa cache, and sets the resident index to None (coarse invalidation, daemon.rs:1188). ONLY the reindex branch is stubbed (daemon.rs:1202 'would spawn background reindex task'). Scope = replace coarse-invalidate + stubbed-reindex with channel-fed delta, not 'write the handler'. (2) TRANSITION GUARD — during cutover, ensure NO dual watcher ownership: the C++ fsnotifier must be disabled when the in-daemon notify watcher is active (don't run both → double events / races). Coordinate with TLDR-ejm.
2026-06-02 CORRECTION (de-stale): TLDR-qr9 is now CLOSED — its interim fix landed (handle_notify invalidation moved into an awaited spawn_blocking). So ac0.2 no longer 'dissolves an open bug'; it SUPERSEDES the interim spawn_blocking with the channel-fed design (watcher -> mpsc -> reindex worker). File:line refs here predate the qr9 commit and may be off by a few lines.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN REFINEMENT (2026-06-02, post-zxb): The watcher feeds dirty events into the existing dirty_files set. Reindex is DEBOUNCED, not instantaneous — idle timeout OR threshold count triggers a batch reindex of only the accumulated dirty files. This prevents "compute always on" — the daemon is quiet while the user is actively editing, and processes changes in one batch when they pause. The zxb commit landed VectorStore in the daemon (Mutex<Option<(Model, VectorStore)>>), so the watcher now has a resident store to apply deltas to. The notify handler already clears the store on any change (coarse invalidation) — ac0.2 replaces this with fine-grained channel-fed delta via t8f.

title: IndexManager: Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> read/write split for concurrent queries + parallel reindex
description: Replace the daemon's coarse Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>> with a thin IndexManager layer owning Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>>. Exposes exactly two methods — query(...) (read lock) and apply_delta(changes) (write lock) — so the daemon and watcher never touch a raw lock. Enables concurrent queries (daemon already spawns task-per-connection at daemon.rs:299; only the index Mutex serializes them today per the daemon.rs:544 comment) and sets up parallel reindex.
design: EVIDENCE (compiler-proven 2026-06-02, see memory concurrency-sync-evidence-2026-06-02): VectorStore + Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> are Send+Sync with ZERO surgery (usearch Index = unsafe impl Send+Sync, all mutators &self; sidecar types Sync). The daemon.rs:122-123 '!Sync' comment is STALE — fix it as part of this work. PATTERN 'compute outside, mutate inside': embedding (slow, rayon-parallel) happens lock-free; only VectorStore::add/remove takes the brief write lock. CAVEATS: (1) read lock MUST be taken inside spawn_blocking, NEVER held across .await (else = qr9 shape); (2) write-lock window is NOT always sub-ms — VectorStore::add triggers reserve() (doubles + reallocates the whole index) on growth. CRATES: parking_lot (cleaner RwLock, no poisoning); arc-swap later only if lock-free reads are measured to matter.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon holds IndexManager<RwLock<VectorStore>> instead of Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>; concurrent queries verified (2+ in-flight take read locks simultaneously); stale !Sync comment removed; read lock only inside spawn_blocking.
notes:
2026-06-02 (Codex review): (1) ADD RESIDENT-LIFECYCLE ACCEPTANCE — today store_search creates a FRESH embedder per query (store_search.rs:202) and the daemon switches models via a single resident SemanticIndex slot (daemon.rs:557). IndexManager must specify: warm Embedder REUSE (no per-query construction), model-scoped store selection, load/rebuild/save timing, and daemon-shutdown behavior. (2) reserve() REALLOC: VectorStore::add grows capacity via reserve() when full (vector_store.rs:200) — a growth realloc under the write lock pauses ALL readers. Add pre-reserve (size to expected corpus + headroom) or batch-delta guidance so growth doesn't unexpectedly stall reads. (3) STALE !Sync COMMENTS to remove are in THREE places, not one: daemon.rs:122, index.rs:159, AND vector_store.rs:91 (claims usearch Index is not Sync).
2026-06-02: the 'remove 3 stale !Sync comments' sub-part is DONE (actually 6 comments; see closed chore TLDR-ac0.4) — that acceptance item is satisfied ahead of ac0.1 proper.
2026-06-02 CORRECTION (de-stale): the 'remove stale !Sync comments' sub-task is COMPLETE (landed in TLDR-ac0.4, committed @ tldr-cli-daemon-support). DISREGARD the earlier 'fix it as part of this work' / 'STALE comments to remove' lines in the design — that acceptance item is satisfied. REMAINING ac0.1 scope = the IndexManager/RwLock<VectorStore> itself (concurrent queries + parallel reindex). NOTE: file:line refs in this issue predate the ac0.4/qr9 commits and may be off by a few lines; locate by symbol, not line number.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: POST-ZXB (2026-06-02): zxb landed VectorStore in the daemon as Mutex<Option<(EmbeddingModel, VectorStore)>>. ac0.1 is NOW UNBLOCKED. The upgrade path: Mutex -> RwLock (or parking_lot RwLock), tuple -> IndexManager struct with query() and apply_delta() methods. The VectorStore is already Send+Sync (proven in TLDR-3il). No surgery needed — just the lock type change and the IndexManager wrapper.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (2026-06-02): IndexManager in daemon/index_manager.rs wraps parking_lot::RwLock<Option<(EmbeddingModel, VectorStore)>>. Three methods: query() (shared read-lock fast path — truly concurrent; write-lock slow path for cold-start/model-switch with re-check), warm() (upgradable read for startup), invalidate() (write-lock clear). Daemon field changed from Arc<Mutex<Option<...>>> to Arc<IndexManager>. All 3 lock sites (warm, semantic query, notify) updated. Tests: concurrent_read_locks_overlap (barrier proves shared reads overlap), upgradable_read_is_exclusive (negative test proves our design avoidance is correct), invalidate_clears_store. 36 existing daemon tests pass. Embedder-reuse (per-query ONNX reload) deferred to TLDR-ac0.5 (P2).

title: Wire search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP search paths (m01 PR2)
description: PR2 of TLDR-m01: replace the in-memory SemanticIndex::build().search() cold path with search_with_store at the three consumers — CLI crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs (~line 153), daemon warm/semantic path crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs (~480-484,569-598), MCP dense path crates/tldr-mcp/src/tools/search.rs (~168-176). Decide the store_dir location here (global cache sibling vs in-tree .tldr/) and ensure the daemon WRITER and cold-CLI READER resolve a BYTE-IDENTICAL path (reuse the daemon's existing per-project hash; §8) or they silently split into two stores. BLOCKED BY TLDR-kkt: must not wire a stale-serving search path before the freshness gate lands.
notes: 2026-06-02 STATUS: 4/5 sub-tasks complete (b1s, 066, h1g, us8). Only buy (MCP+hybrid, P2) remains. Core wiring shipped and pushed. ac0.1 is unblocked.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: TWO REQUIREMENTS from the kkt Codex review (must be handled when wiring): (1) STORE-LOCATION GUARD (Item 5): store_dir + cache_dir MUST be outside the indexed corpus or the freshness gate sees the store's own writes as drift -> rebuild-always. Documented as a precondition in store_search, but UNDEFENDED in code. Add a call-site assertion/check that the resolved store_dir/cache_dir is not a non-skipped subdir of root (CacheConfig::default = ~/.cache/tldr/embeddings is 'usually' outside but not guaranteed for every root). (2) PER-QUERY WALK COST (Item 6): compute_corpus_digest walks+stats the WHOLE tree on every search call in the current helper; a huge monorepo pays a full stat-walk even on a cache hit (and a cold miss pays it twice). When wiring the resident daemon, move the freshness check to LOAD/notify time (compute once on load, re-check on fs-notify), NOT per query. The cold CLI can keep per-call (one query).

comments.text: ZXB LANDED (2026-06-02, commit pushed to remote). 4/5 sub-tasks done: b1s (fallback removal), 066 (CLI cold search), h1g (embed), us8 (daemon warm+query). Only buy (zxb.5, MCP+hybrid, P2) remains. Key deliverables: (1) store_search.rs refactored into search_with_store + load_or_build_store + query_store. (2) store_dir_for() in types.rs for shared daemon/CLI path resolution (~/.cache/tldr/stores/<hash>/). (3) Daemon holds Mutex<Option<(EmbeddingModel, VectorStore)>> — warm is near-instant on fresh store. (4) SemanticIndex fallback removed per TLDR-lx7. ac0.1 is now UNBLOCKED.

title: Store freshness gate: detect source drift -> full rebuild (m01 reconcile-lite)
description: Codex review of m01 PR1 (commit d874b77): a loaded VectorStore is served as-is — VectorStore::load verifies persisted manifest/index integrity but does NOT check whether SOURCE files changed since the store was built. The manifest only invalidates on model/chunk-param/walker change, so search_with_store is currently build-once-until-config-changes: after a source edit it serves STALE vectors/rankings (and read_snippet reads current source lines against outdated line-range metadata). This is the design-doc §7.3 RECONCILE: on load, stat each persisted FileRecord {mtime,size,file_type} vs disk; on ANY drift (changed/added/removed/type-swapped file) do a FULL REBUILD. DISTINCT from TLDR-t8f, which is the incremental delta OPTIMIZATION (add/remove changed chunks without full re-embed); this is the coarse correctness gate t8f later optimizes. The store already persists FileRecords (vector_store.rs set_file_record/file_record) — the gate must enumerate the corpus the SAME way the chunker/walker does (same skip rules) or it rebuilds-always / misses deletions. HARD BLOCKER for wiring search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP (PR2).
close_reason: DONE (commits 8a19b09 + TOCTOU fix). Corpus-digest freshness gate implemented, tested (pure: determinism/add/edit/delete/0-chunk-trap; e2e real-model: rebuild-only-on-drift), and Codex-reviewed: 4 CORRECT, Item 3 TOCTOU BUG fixed (digest snapshot before chunk), Item 7 docs fixed. Items 5 (call-site store-location guard) + 6 (per-query walk -> load/notify time) deferred to zxb where wiring handles them. store_search now rebuilds on source drift instead of serving stale rankings. Unblocks zxb.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED + Codex-reviewed (commit 8a19b09 + TOCTOU fix). Corpus-digest freshness gate: chunker::enumerate_corpus_files (shared pre-parse enumeration), compute_corpus_digest (FNV over sorted rel-path+mtime+size, stat-only), Manifest.corpus_digest (STORE_FORMAT_VERSION 2->3), store_search rebuilds when stored!=current. Codex verdicts: digest correctness CORRECT, 0-chunk trap CORRECT, format/serde CORRECT, chunk_directory refactor no-drift CORRECT. FIXED: Item 3 BUG (TOCTOU) — moved digest snapshot to BEFORE chunk_code so it describes the source state the vectors were built from (a mid-build edit now skews toward rebuild, not stale-serve); Item 7 stale 'not-freshness-checked' docs updated. DEFERRED to zxb (commented there): Item 5 (call-site store-location guard) + Item 6 (move per-query walk to load/notify time for the daemon). Tests: pure digest (determinism + add/edit/delete + 0-chunk trap) + e2e (real model, rebuild-only-on-drift via generation counting) — all pass; the e2e caught a store-inside-corpus gotcha now documented as a hard zxb precondition. NEXT: zxb (wire into CLI/daemon/MCP) is now UNBLOCKED.

title: Wire real ManifestId inputs (model_revision / chunk_params / walker_version)
description: Codex re-review (TLDR-l5d) §4: ManifestId currently carries PLACEHOLDER inputs — model_revision='rev-1', chunk_params='fn', walker_version='w1' (see manifest_id test + production build path). These must reflect REAL values (embedding tokenizer+weights revision, actual chunker parameters, walker/AST version) before the store ships, otherwise a model/recipe/walker change will NOT invalidate the on-disk store and load() will serve stale vectors as 'compatible'. The typed-LoadFail Incompatible path (b168c13) only protects config changes that are actually REFLECTED in ManifestId — placeholders defeat it.
close_reason: RESOLVED in m01 PR1 (commit d874b77). store_search::manifest_id_for now builds a REAL ManifestId via ManifestId::for_build(model, root, chunk_params_tag(opts), CHUNK_WALKER_VERSION): model_revision/dimensions/embed_schema were already real (embed_schema_tag reads TLDR_ENRICH); the two placeholders (chunk_params, walker_version) are now wired — chunk_params_tag = gran+sorted-langs, CHUNK_WALKER_VERSION='w1' (a single bump-on-chunking-change const). Unit-tested by manifest_id_reflects_model_and_chunk_inputs (model/granularity/language change all flip identity -> LoadFail::Incompatible -> rebuild). Codex-verified (no enrich staleness hole).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE REFINEMENT (Codex 2026-06-02): narrower than filed. ManifestId::for_build (vector_store.rs:1010) ALREADY derives model_revision=model.model_name(), dimensions, embed_schema for real. The ONLY placeholders are the caller-supplied chunk_params and walker_version args. FIX = pass real values at the m01 wiring call site: chunk_params from granularity+languages (the ChunkOptions actually used), walker_version from the AST/walker version constant. Resolve INLINE within m01's first PR (it's a correctness prerequisite — without it a model/recipe/walker change won't invalidate the store). NOT a separate blocking PR.

title: Cache key strip_prefix silently falls back to raw path (can revert the absolute-vs-relative bug)
description: Codex review (2026-06-02, thread a708b3161b2e63d89). CacheKey::from_chunk (crates/tldr-core/src/semantic/cache.rs) strips the build root via strip_prefix and on FAILURE falls back to chunk.file_path.to_string_lossy() VERBATIM (the raw as-given path). For a symlinked root, a differently-normalized path, or a chunk outside the root, this silently re-creates the original absolute-vs-relative key-divergence (the TLDR-atc bug -> re-embed). walker.rs does NOT canonicalize (stores root.as_ref()) and the chunker stores path.to_path_buf(), so the 'all chunk paths are root-prefixed' assumption is unverified at the type level. FIX: canonicalize the root once + derive file_rel_path deterministically; on strip_prefix miss, log a WARNING and use a single canonical fallback (canonical absolute path), never the raw path. Same rule applies to the future usearch keys (l5d). Introduced with TLDR-atc cache-key fix (401a7ac).
close_reason: FIXED 2026-06-02 (commits 987af50 + 2d72df2). The silent strip_prefix raw-path fallback is hardened in BOTH locations: vector_store.rs root_relative (987af50) and cache.rs CacheKey::from_chunk via key_rel_path (2d72df2). Pattern: lexical strip -> canonical strip -> canonical-absolute (warned) -> raw (warned) — never a silent revert to the absolute-vs-relative divergence. Empty key_root (default/tests) still lexically matches -> full path unchanged. cache+vector_store tests pass.

title: Daemon handle_notify takes the semantic-index sync Mutex on the async thread (blocks runtime)
description: Codex review (2026-06-02, thread a708b3161b2e63d89). handle_notify (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs) acquires the semantic_index Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>> DIRECTLY in async code to invalidate it. The Semantic handler holds that same mutex across the whole build+search INSIDE spawn_blocking (correct). But a Notify arriving mid-build calls .lock() on the async thread and PARKS a Tokio worker for the entire build (seconds), defeating the spawn_blocking responsiveness fix (commit 7199f03). FIX: Notify must not lock the index on the async path — signal invalidation via an AtomicBool/mpsc channel (or do the lock inside spawn_blocking), keeping handle_notify O(1)/lock-free. See docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md section 8. Introduced with TLDR-atc spawn_blocking work.
notes:
2026-06-02: Do NOT fix in isolation. Route 2 chosen — TLDR-ac0.2 (in-daemon notify watcher) dissolves this by construction: invalidation moves to an internal mpsc channel, the async path never locks the index. Fix as part of ac0.2.
2026-06-02 (Codex review): RESOLVE AMBIGUITY. Two valid paths — (A) cheap interim, independently shippable NOW: move invalidation off the async path per this issue's original FIX (AtomicBool/mpsc; or take the lock inside spawn_blocking), keeping handle_notify O(1)/lock-free; (B) TLDR-ac0.2 supersedes by removing the IPC Notify path entirely. Prefer A if qr9 bites before ac0.2 lands (it is a live P1 runtime-blocking risk: handle_notify locks the sync mutex on the async path at daemon.rs:1188 while build/search holds it through blocking work at daemon.rs:550). If ac0.2 lands first, close qr9 as folded. NOT blocked by ac0.2 — A stays actionable.
close_reason: FIXED (interim, Codex before+after reviewed). handle_notify no longer locks the semantic_index std::sync::Mutex on the async path — moved to an awaited tokio::task::spawn_blocking (Arc::clone, poison-tolerant). Async worker no longer parked during an in-flight build. Ordering caveat + the ac0.2 superseding path documented inline. Build clean; 18 notify/daemon tests pass incl. test_semantic_index_invalidated_on_notify. Uncommitted in working tree (user controls commit/push). Full event-driven replacement remains TLDR-ac0.2.

title: Expand semantic eval gold set 14 -> 50-100 cases (single-case swings make all deltas untrustworthy)
description: The semantic_eval harness has 14 gold (query->file/fn) cases. At n=14, one case = 0.071 recall, so model/feature comparisons swing on a SINGLE query: e.g. ArcticL-vs-ArcticM looked like 'L is worse @5' but the entire difference was ONE query (parse imports rank 1->8) — i.e. within noise. This makes it impossible to trust the 4er-hybrid lift, the 9m2-descriptions lift, or any model choice. FIX: grow the gold set to 50-100 query->target pairs spanning the static layers (ast/cfg/dfg/callgraph), search, and semantic subsystems, with a mix of easy (lexically-obvious) and hard (modality-gap) queries, so recall@5/@10/MRR deltas become statistically meaningful. Gate trustworthy measurement of TLDR-4er and TLDR-9m2 on this.
acceptance_criteria: Gold set >=50 cases; a single-query rank change moves recall by <0.02; 4er/9m2 lifts measured against the expanded set with the deltas exceeding single-case noise.
close_reason: Gold-set expansion DONE. semantic_eval GOLD is now 52 cases (was 14) spanning ast/cfg/dfg/callgraph/search/security/quality/semantic subsystems, easy + modality-gap queries (semantic_eval.rs:28-294, comment credits TLDR-6h3). Acceptance met: >=50 cases (52); single-query swing = 1/52 = 0.019 < 0.02. Latest run (2026-06-02): R@5 0.750, R@10 0.827, MRR 0.599. The remaining acceptance clause ('measure 4er/9m2 lifts exceeding single-case noise') is a USE of this now-stable harness by those features when they land (TLDR-4er hybrid, TLDR-9m2 descriptions) — not remaining work on the gold set itself.

title: Incremental re-indexing: delta add/remove on file change instead of full re-embed
description:
Today every index build re-chunks the whole tree and re-embeds anything whose content-hash changed; a full rebuild is slow. usearch supports remove(key)/add(key) on a writable RAM index, enabling INCREMENTAL updates: on a file change, re-chunk ONLY that file, remove its stale function-keys, add the changed ones — a few-ms-per-file delta instead of a full rebuild. REQUIRES a RAM-resident writable index (view() is read-only #97) -> lives in the daemon (TLDR-atc, DONE) on the usearch store (TLDR-l5d).

FULL DESIGN: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md.

Key scheme (RECONCILED with the doc — supersedes the earlier 'path+name+signature hash' wording): stable u64 = hash("{file_rel_path}::{class_name}::{function_name}::{ordinal}"), root-relative; ordinal disambiguates duplicate names within a file; file-level chunks use "{file_rel_path}#file". (Ordinal is positional -> same-name insert churns the key, but bounded by the content-addressed dedup layer = cache hit, no real re-embed.)

Delta classifier: EMBED (body content_hash changed) / META-ONLY (unchanged body, line shift) / REMOVE (deleted). Debounce: 750ms quiet + 5s max-wait + source-only filter + burst cap (200 files / 1000 events per 2s) -> full rebuild. handle_notify MUST stay lock-free on the async thread (see TLDR-qr9). Reconcile on restart = per-file mtime+size vs stored record (no all-files hashing).

DEPENDS ON l5d shipping the sidecar + manifest + per-file records: the delta needs per-file key lookup + content_hash tracking that can't re-chunk the whole tree, which is exactly why those persist in l5d (P0), not t8f.
acceptance_criteria: Editing one file re-embeds only that file's changed functions (not the corpus); deleted functions' vectors are removed; query results reflect the change within one daemon save cycle; full rebuild still available as fallback.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Design doc written: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (committed 73cf41f). Covers stable u64 chunk keys + metadata sidecar + per-file key index, the embed/meta-only/remove delta classifier, debounce/coalesce pipeline w/ burst cap, atomic save + startup reconcile, spawn_blocking+Mutex concurrency, edge cases, full-rebuild fallback, 4-phase rollout (P0 usearch store=l5d -> P1 delta core -> P2 debounce -> P3 persistence). Gated on l5d (usearch writable RAM index; view() read-only NOT used at our scale).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN REFINEMENT (2026-06-02, post-zxb): Debounced batch reindex is the confirmed flow. NOT instantaneous per-save. Flow: file save -> add to dirty set -> threshold hit OR idle timeout -> batch reindex only the dirty files. This keeps compute bursty (process accumulated changes when user pauses) not constant (no re-embed on every keystroke). The ONNX model stays loaded in the daemon so there is zero per-batch startup cost. A few changed chunks embed in milliseconds. The dirty_files HashSet + auto_reindex_threshold already exist in daemon.rs — t8f builds the delta logic on top of that accumulation. Key parameters: debounce quiet=750ms, max-wait=5s (from design doc), burst cap=200 files (triggers full rebuild instead of delta).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: P1 delta core LANDED (commit b084d7a). Implementation: shared key_chunks() guarantees build/delta key parity; VectorStore::apply_file_delta (remove/EMBED/META-ONLY classify + stale-snapshot re-validation under write lock) + apply_file_delete; IndexManager::apply_delta classifies under read lock, embeds lock-free on a resident Embedder (no per-save ONNX reload), applies under write lock; handle_notify calls it in spawn_blocking with invalidate() fallback on Err/NeedsRebuild (incl. TLDR_ENRICH). Deltas are RAM-only (daemon never persists the store; restart reloads pre-delta disk store, freshness gate rebuilds). Tests: 3 new delta unit tests in vector_store + rewritten notify test now asserts delta PRESERVES warm-state; full core lib 4882 pass, cli notify suite green, clippy clean. ACCEPTANCE MET (re-embed only changed funcs / deleted vectors removed / reflects within a save cycle / full-rebuild fallback). REMAINING in t8f scope: P2 debounce/coalesce (750ms quiet + 5s max-wait + source-only filter + 200-file/1000-event burst cap -> full rebuild) on top of the existing dirty_files set. P3 incremental persistence is separate.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ADVISOR REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (verified, commit pending push): Closed the deletion-path gap — deletes now route through deleted_file_rel() (lexical strip vs project + its canonical form, since a gone file can't canonicalize), so deletions match the build's keys even under a symlinked root (ss3 bug class). Added end-to-end test through handle_notify proving EDIT keeps the vector count (no phantom/orphan keys = build/delta key parity) and DELETE drops to zero — both the criteria a warmth-only check can't see. Passes against a real warmed ONNX store (145s). REMAINING t8f scope (P2): (1) source-files-only FILTER on the delta path — design §6 first gate; currently a Notify for a non-corpus/ignored file (node_modules, generated, unknown ext) runs chunk_file -> empty chunks -> writes an empty FileRecord, a CORRECTNESS divergence from the walker, not just perf; (2) debounce/coalesce (750ms quiet + 5s max-wait + 200-file/1000-event/2s burst cap -> full rebuild) on the existing dirty_files set. P3 incremental persistence separate.

title: DECISION: Storage access split — read-only view() for queries, load()+RAM for mutation
description: FINDING (usearch issue #97, maintainer): a view()'d memory-mapped on-disk index is READ-ONLY — you cannot add() to it (the future 'merge' feature aside). DECISION: COLD CLI query path uses view() (mmap, low RSS, fast, no full load). UPDATE/WRITE path uses load() into a writable RAM-resident Index, then add/remove/save(). These are two distinct access modes over the same .usearch file. Consequence: incremental updates cannot happen against the mmap'd file directly — they need the RAM index (see daemon decision).
notes: 2026-06-02 (Codex review): CLARIFIED/PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED by the fully-in-memory decision (memory in-memory-index-decision-2026-06-02). The daemon/warm path is now RAM-resident (load() into RwLock<VectorStore>, mutate in place — store_search.rs:182 already load()s into RAM), NOT view()/mmap. zl8's view()-for-queries holds ONLY for the cold-CLI no-daemon path, and even that becomes optional if cold CLI also load()s into RAM. Not reopening — recording the scope narrowing.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: Search algorithm = exact brute-force (exact_search_f32), HNSW deliberately NOT used
description: CONTEXT: usearch's headline feature is HNSW approximate ANN. CHOSEN: use exact_search_f32 (brute force), NOT search_f32 (HNSW). RATIONALE: usearch docs say HNSW is for 'millions of entries'; exact is recommended for 'smaller collections'. tldr targets <=100K LOC = 14K-117K vectors. Exact gives 100% recall (vs HNSW 99.3%), and the scan was never the bottleneck (0.1ms; the LOAD was). add() still builds an HNSW graph (~1.5s @117K, negligible vs 30min embed) but we never query it — acceptable; optionally set low connectivity to trim graph RAM. REVISIT only if corpus scale reaches ~1M+ vectors. This is vendor-endorsed config, not a limitation.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: Vector store backend = usearch (exact mode), not hand-rolled mmap
description: CONTEXT: semantic search's JSON cache (1.1GB, 4.3s cold parse on EVERY query — no daemon route) needed replacing. Two candidates: plain memmap2+bincode (pure Rust) vs usearch (cxx->C++). CHOSEN: usearch. RATIONALE: usearch view() = mmap load (kills 4.3s), exact_search_f32 = exact brute-force (100% recall, 20x faster than FAISS flat), save/load = binary persistence, ScalarKind = optional quantization — i.e. it delivers the exact goal as a MAINTAINED library, collapsing ~8 hand-rolled children into one integration. The earlier anti-usearch premise ('HNSW is approximate, we need exact') was FALSE: exact_search exists. COST ACCEPTED: C++ compiler in build/CI (cxx bridge). Supersedes TLDR-7kf/8pt/k4q/j8c. See memory usearch-discovery / decision-locked.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Eval harness benchmarks ArcticM (code default) but users run their configured model (e.g. ArcticL) — benchmark doesn't measure what's deployed
description: The semantic_eval harness builds its index with Some(Default::default()) (semantic_eval.rs:121) -> EmbedOptions::default() -> EmbeddingModel::default() = ArcticM (768-dim, the #[default] enum variant). It NEVER reads ~/.tldr/config.json. The real CLI path uses EmbeddingModel::resolve(config) which DOES read config. On this machine the global config sets model=arctic-l (1024-dim), so the user's actual 'tldr semantic' runs ArcticL while the benchmark runs ArcticM. CONSEQUENCE: the prefix verdict (recall@5 0.071->0.857, MRR 0.685) is measured on ArcticM and is UNVALIDATED for the model the user actually deploys. The handoff's claim 'benchmark with the PRODUCTION model ArcticM' was imprecise — ArcticM is the shipped enum default, not necessarily the user's configured/production model. There is no beads decision establishing ArcticM as 'production' (searched). FIX: make the eval resolve the model the same way the CLI does (EmbeddingModel::resolve via TldrConfig::resolve), OR accept a model arg/env so the benchmark can target the deployed model. Then re-run the prefix verdict on ArcticL to validate. Also revisit which model is the intended default/production target — separate product decision.
acceptance_criteria: Eval harness benchmarks the same model the CLI resolves (config-aware or explicit arg); prefix verdict re-confirmed on ArcticL; intended production/default model documented.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm. Measurement-validity gap surfaced during the prefix-verdict dogfooding. Pairs with TLDR-h27 (threshold) — both are 'eval passes but CLI behaves differently' gaps: eval hardcodes ArcticM + threshold 0.0, CLI uses ArcticL + threshold 0.50.
close_reason: semantic_eval now resolves the deployed model via TldrConfig::resolve (matches the CLI), with TLDR_EVAL_MODEL override, instead of the hardcoded EmbeddingModel::default() (ArcticM). Eval now benchmarks the model users actually run (e.g. ArcticL). semantic_eval.rs.

title: LLM-generated function descriptions for embedding (summary-augmented retrieval)
description: Highest-leverage quality lever for semantic search. At index time, call a cheap/fast LLM (e.g. Haiku) per chunk to generate a 1-line natural-language description of what the function does, then embed THAT (alongside or instead of code). Attacks the root cause: queries are English but raw code lacks query words; an English description bridges the gap (e.g. 'combines two ranked lists via reciprocal rank fusion' matches a 'combine two rankings' query). Known RAG pattern (document/summary augmentation; cousin of HyDE). NEITHER llm-tldr (python) NOR tldr-core has attempted this (verified by grep). User will provide LLM API key.
design: Indexing stage: (1) per-chunk LLM call -> 1-line description; (2) CACHE keyed by content_hash so we pay once per function and only re-summarize changed functions (mirror the embedding cache's incremental model); (3) feed description into build_embedding_text. Cost: ~one cheap call per function, one-time + incremental, fully cached. Provider/key via existing config (config.rs already has embedding provider/api_key_env scaffolding). DEFERRED to a later iteration per user; this task captures it.
acceptance_criteria: Per-chunk descriptions generated via configurable LLM provider; cached by content hash; included in embedding text; measurable recall@5/MRR lift vs the prefix+enrichment baseline on the eval harness.
notes: SEQUENCING (2026-06-01): TLDR-d8f (embed EXISTING docstrings, free, no API key) is the cheaper precursor — do it first and measure. 9m2's LLM-generated descriptions then target the functions that LACK docstrings (or to augment thin ones). Both attack the same modality gap; d8f is the zero-cost slice, 9m2 the full-coverage version. Still needs your LLM API key (Haiku per spec).

title: Phase 1b: set Arctic query/passage prefix in embed + search
description: EmbedOptions::use_prefix (embedder.rs:57) exists but is never set. Arctic models need asymmetric prefixes: passage prefix for documents (build), query prefix for the search query (index.rs:375). Free recall win. Validate exact prefix strings against fastembed/Arctic docs.
close_reason: Arctic query prefix shipped in e9e5427 (embedder.rs embed_query + EmbeddingModel::query_prefix), default-ON via TLDR_QUERY_PREFIX. Full-scale eval verdict: recall@5 0.071->0.857, MRR 0.083->0.685 on crates/tldr-core/src. Decisive win, stays default-on.

title: Phase 1a: wire enrichment.rs into SemanticIndex::build
description: enrichment.rs (build_embedding_text, 8-field recipe matching the Python original) is fully implemented but NEVER called. index.rs:310-313 embeds raw chunk.content. Wire enrich_chunks+build_embedding_text into the build path. MUST bump cache key with an embed-recipe version tag (e.g. embed_schema=enriched-v1) so stale raw-embedded vectors aren't reused. Biggest quality lever; no new dependency.
notes:
QUADRATIC FIXED: enrichment re-parsed each file ~3x PER FUNCTION via get_cfg_context/get_dfg_context. Now FileAnalysisCache parses each file ONCE (file_trees) and CFG/DFG reuse it via extract_cfg_from_tree + extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg (the pre-parsed helpers that already existed for vuln scanning). Result: enrich-on over 272 chunks = 16.7s (was: hung 12min/12GB on full crate). 

MEASURED (semantic/ scope, 8 cases, prefix on): enrich OFF -> R@5 0.875/MRR 0.875; enrich ON (with CFG/DFG) -> R@5 0.875/MRR 0.708. Enrichment did NOT help here — slightly hurt ranking. Tempers the 'enrichment is the biggest lever' hypothesis. CAVEATS: small/noisy scope; CFG/DFG-on variant only (cheap signal-only untested, blocked by cache tag not capturing flow flag); full-corpus untested. Enrichment stays GATED OFF by default. Prefix remains the only proven win so far.
close_reason: Enrichment wired into SemanticIndex::build + per-function re-parse quadratic fixed (reuses extract_cfg_from_tree/extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg), shipped 37ab719+e9e5427. Gated OFF by default (TLDR_ENRICH) because at semantic/ scope enrichment did NOT help recall (MRR 0.875->0.708 w/ CFG/DFG). Module now compiled (mod decl added) + embed_schema_version pins raw-v1 vs enriched-v1 cache keys.

title: Phase 0: semantic search eval harness (recall@5 / MRR gold set)
description: Hand-author ~12-15 (query -> expected file+function) cases over THIS codebase and compute recall@5 + MRR, so semantic-search quality is measurable before/after Phase 1 changes. Drives existing SemanticIndex::build+search. Capture baseline before any change.
notes: BASELINE (crates/tldr-core/src, ArcticM, raw-source embeddings, no query prefix, no hybrid): Recall@5=0.143 (2/14), Recall@10=0.143, MRR=0.086. Only 'generate embeddings for a batch' (rank 1) and 'reaching definitions' (rank 5) hit. Even 'cosine similarity' misses top-10. Empirically confirms search is broken. Harness: crates/tldr-core/examples/semantic_eval.rs (cargo run -p tldr-core --release --example semantic_eval).
close_reason: Eval harness built + baseline captured (R@5=0.143, MRR=0.086). Measurement vehicle for Phase 1.

title: Results-equivalence verification harness
description: The real pass/fail gate. Replay the captured baseline queries (tests 1-7 outputs: scores + snippets, the golden reference) against the new mmap-backed build and DIFF the ranked results. With brute-force + f32 kept, results must be IDENTICAL. Also capture cold-latency old vs new to confirm the ~710ms single-file target. Latency improvement is necessary but NOT sufficient -- equivalence is the gate. Record results on the epic.
notes: REFRAMED: verify usearch exact_search_f32 returns results IDENTICAL to the current brute-force top_k_similar over the captured baseline (scores+order). This is the PASS/FAIL gate (results-equivalence, not latency) — usearch exact mode must match, else investigate metric/normalization (cosine vs IP; vectors are unit-normalized already).
close_reason: Equivalence GATE PROVEN (the stated pass/fail). semantic_eval store-equivalence harness (TLDR_EVAL_STORE=1) diffs usearch exact_search_f32 vs dense top_k_similar over n=52 gold: 52/52 equivalent, REAL=0, EPSILON=0, UNPROVEN=0 (2026-06-02), confirmed across 3 Codex passes. Removed the stale 2a4->m01 dependency: equivalence was verified DIRECTLY (VectorStore vs SemanticIndex) and did NOT require the m01 production rewire. Secondary cold-latency capture (JSON-load vs store-load) folded into TLDR-zxb (measurable only once the store serves production).

title: Rewire EmbeddingCache onto usearch Index
description:
Rewire EmbeddingCache (cache.rs) onto the usearch-backed store (TLDR-l5d) while keeping the PUBLIC API stable (open/get/put/flush/len/is_empty) so index.rs / similarity.rs need ZERO changes. (REFRAMED from the obsolete 'MmapVectorStore' wording — we use the usearch library via l5d.)

KEY POINT — EmbeddingCache IS the content-addressed dedup layer (design doc §4.1):
- Keep get(chunk, model) / put(chunk, embedding, model) keyed by (content_hash, model, embed_schema) so an UNCHANGED body (revert, rename, ordinal-churn) is a CACHE HIT, never a re-embed.
- This dedup layer is DISTINCT from the key(u64)->vector usearch SEARCH index that l5d builds. m01 makes EmbeddingCache persist/dedup vectors via the usearch store instead of the 1.4GB JSON HashMap (cache.rs load_with_lock). Do NOT collapse the two or drop content-hash dedup.

- get/put/flush delegate to the l5d store; signatures unchanged so callers (index.rs build/search/find_similar) compile and behave identically.
- GC = reachability-on-full-reindex (drop keys not re-added) per the locked decision (TLDR-rgc); cached_at/file_mtime droppable once reachability-GC lands.

Depends on TLDR-l5d. Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md.
acceptance_criteria: EmbeddingCache get/put/flush/open/len/is_empty signatures UNCHANGED; index.rs build()/search()/find_similar() compile + behave identically (results IDENTICAL on the n=52 eval + captured baselines); vectors persist/dedup via the usearch store, NOT the JSON HashMap; content-hash dedup preserved (unchanged body on rekey = cache hit, no re-embed); 1.4GB cache.json path removed; reachability-GC drops orphaned keys on full reindex.
notes: REFRAMED mmap->usearch. EmbeddingCache.get/put/flush delegate to the usearch wrapper (TLDR-l5d) instead of the JSON HashMap (cache.rs load_with_lock:236). Keep the get/put/flush signatures so callers (index.rs) are untouched.
close_reason: Core goal achieved: VectorStore is the production search path (PR1 store_search helper + PR2 zxb wiring). EmbeddingCache format migration to follow in TLDR-3gj.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: M01 PLAN (Codex-reviewed, code-grounded 2026-06-02). STRATEGY: load() into RAM (NOT view()/mmap — no view path exists yet; mmap pulls in unimplemented reader-grace = TLDR-pdb, deferred). SemanticIndex is already RAM-backed brute-force, so parity is natural. SMALLEST SAFE PR: add ONE shared store-backed helper that (1) derives a REAL ManifestId::for_build (resolves 7al inline — pass real chunk_params=granularity+languages, walker_version; model_revision/dims/embed_schema already real), (2) tries VectorStore::load(), (3) on miss/LoadFail builds+saves then searches, (4) FALLS BACK to SemanticIndex::build().search() on ANY store I/O error (never surface to user). Wire into: CLI cold path crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs:~153; daemon warm/semantic path daemon.rs:~480-484,569-598; MCP dense path crates/tldr-mcp/src/tools/search.rs:~168-176. LEAVE cache.rs UNTOUCHED — EmbeddingCache stays the content-addressed (file,fn,content_hash,model+schema)->vec dedup layer, consumed only inside VectorStore::build; do NOT change get/put or JSON format. TOP FAILURE MODES to test: (1) SCORE INVERSION — SearchHit.distance is cosine DISTANCE (lower=better); SemanticIndex reports SIMILARITY; convert score=1-distance BEFORE thresholds. (2) SNIPPETS — ChunkMeta has lines/hash not content; must lazy-read by file/line or keep SemanticIndex for snippet paths. (3) MANIFEST staleness — test model-change AND granularity/language-change both trigger LoadFail::Incompatible. (4) PATH-KEY drift — test relative vs absolute/canonical roots (set_key_root/root_relative). (5) FALLBACK — every LoadFail variant (no-gen, checksum drift, torn CURRENT, missing sidecar) must rebuild-and-save, not error. DEFERRED to follow-ups: view()/mmap, pdb reader-grace, incremental deltas (t8f), 'similar' replacement, EmbeddingCache format changes, HNSW/i8, deleting SemanticIndex.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: PR1 LANDED (branch tldr-m01-store-wiring; commits d874b77, f556d0b, 184108b). New module crates/tldr-core/src/semantic/store_search.rs — search_with_store(): first production caller of VectorStore::load. Flow: load()->on any failure REBUILD via VectorStore::build + best-effort save(); build/embed/search error -> fall back to SemanticIndex; save error -> warn+serve in-RAM. title: Integrate usearch as vector store (add_f32 / exact_search_f32 / save / load) + key->metadata sidecar
description:
FIRST PASS = full-rebuild usearch store (NO incremental; incremental = TLDR-t8f). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (§3 store, §4 data model, §7 persistence). Supersedes the hand-rolled memmap2+bincode 'MmapVectorStore' (TLDR-j8c) — we use the usearch LIBRARY.

VECTOR STORE — usearch NativeIndex, key(u64) -> vector:
- add_f32(key,&[f32]) on build; exact_search_f32 for query (exact KNN, NOT HNSW). f32 ONLY (i8 = TLDR-ccg).
- load()/save() into a WRITABLE RAM index, NOT view() (view() is read-only #97; at ~58MB load() is cheap and keeps it writable for t8f). load() is scale-bounded (revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo). Metric cos (unit-normalized).

CONTENT-ADDRESSED DEDUP layer — kept DISTINCT from key->vector: (content_hash, model, embed_schema) -> vector, so renames/ordinal-churn re-add WITHOUT re-embedding (the current EmbeddingCache role; do not drop it).

SIDECAR + PER-FILE record — usearch stores ONLY vectors; everything else lives here:
- key -> ChunkMeta {identity, file_rel_path, function_name, class_name, line_start, line_end, content_hash}.
- file_rel_path -> FileRecord {keys, mtime, size, file_type}.
- Snippets are read LAZILY from source at query time (single buffered read -> hash that buffer -> render; validated by content_hash; degrade to no-snippet on missing/changed/out-of-range), NOT stored.

MANIFEST (one per store) covering every input that changes vectors OR chunk boundaries: format_version, generation, embedding_model, model_revision (tokenizer+weights), dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, search_mode, embed_schema, chunk_params, walker_version, root, chunk_count, keys_checksum (key-set digest), index_checksum (index FILE bytes), sidecar_checksum. load() REJECTS -> full rebuild on ANY mismatch.

CRASH-SAFE SAVE (doc §7.1 — NOT 'temp+rename then manifest last', which clobbers the live pair): generation-suffixed IMMUTABLE files index.<gen>.usearch / meta.<gen> / manifest.<gen>; a structured CURRENT {magic, gen, checksum} pointer whose atomic rename is the SOLE commit point; fsync files AND dir; GC retains KEEP_GENS (3) + a grace window (60s), never the gen CURRENT points at. On invalid/torn CURRENT, scan manifest.<gen> newest->oldest for the newest verifying trio. Single-writer (daemon) / multi-reader (incl. the cold CLI): readers snapshot CURRENT, open the immutable trio, retry on a GC'd file.

HARDEN the root-relative key: strip_prefix failure must WARN + use a canonical fallback, never the silent raw-path fallback that reverts the atc bug (tracked as TLDR-ss3).

OUTCOME vs the 1.4GB JSON cache.json: ~58MB f32 binary + small sidecar; daemon RAM ~2.3GB -> ~0.5GB; first-load ~14s -> sub-second.
acceptance_criteria: usearch store builds the full index (add_f32) and searches (exact_search_f32) with results IDENTICAL to the JSON-cache path on the n=52 eval + captured baselines; content-addressed dedup layer prevents re-embedding unchanged bodies on rekey/rename; sidecar resolves key->{path,fn,class,line range,content_hash} + per-file {keys,mtime,size,file_type}; MANIFEST gates load (any model/model_revision/dims/scalar/search_mode/embed_schema/chunk_params/walker_version/root mismatch, OR keys_/index_/sidecar_checksum failure, OR generation mismatch -> full rebuild, never silent); crash-safe generation+CURRENT save survives SIGKILL at any point and a torn CURRENT falls back to the newest verifying manifest.<gen>; concurrent cold-CLI reader + daemon writer never see a torn/half store; first-load ~14s -> sub-second and daemon RSS drops materially (1.4GB JSON -> ~58MB f32); f32 only (i8 = TLDR-ccg); NO incremental (full rebuild only; incremental = TLDR-t8f); cache.json path seam-switched/removed.
notes:
USEARCH FEATURE GUIDANCE (research 2026-06-01): CORE = view() [read-only mmap, query path] + exact_search_f32 [100% recall, 20x faster than FAISS flat, vendor-recommended for <1M vectors] + save() + add(). add() builds an unused HNSW graph (~1.5s @117K, negligible) — fine; optionally set low connectivity to trim graph RAM. KEY CONSTRAINT (issue#97): view() is READ-ONLY — you canNOT add to a mmap'd on-disk index. So: QUERY path = view()+exact_search (low RSS); UPDATE path = load() into RAM + add/remove + save(). EXPLOIT LATER: i8 ScalarKind quantization (near-lossless for cosine+unit-norm, 4x smaller) as opt-in compact mode; filtered_search_f32 predicate for scoped/path-filtered queries. Metric = Cos (vectors already unit-normalized — could also use IP since normalized).
2026-06-01 RESCOPED to match docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (committed 73cf41f): use usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index, NOT view()/mmap (view() is read-only issue#97 and unneeded at ~58MB f32/~15MB i8 — load() is cheap and keeps the index writable, the prereq for t8f). f32 FIRST; i8 quantization is opt-in later (ccg). Metadata SIDECAR is required (usearch stores vectors only). Full-rebuild only this pass. The OLD description (hand-rolled memmap2+bincode 'MmapVectorStore' via j8c) is OBSOLETE — the usearch library supersedes it. Prior USEARCH FEATURE GUIDANCE notes remain valid for the API.
2026-06-02 CODEX REVIEW (thread a708b3161b2e63d89) — KEEP the sidecar IN l5d; do NOT defer it to t8f (an earlier reconciliation idea — REJECTED). Rationale: a persisted usearch index is vectors-only, so after a daemon RESTART, search results (path/fn/line range/snippet) cannot be reconstructed without the sidecar, and re-chunking the whole tree on every restart is exactly the cost we remove. So l5d ships: usearch vector store + sidecar + MANIFEST. ADD to scope: (1) Store MANIFEST {format_version, generation, embedding_model, dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, embed_schema, root, chunk_count, checksum}; load() REJECTS->full-rebuild on any mismatch (model/dims/scalar/schema/root/format or index-vs-manifest count/dims or checksum or index-vs-sidecar generation). (2) PAIRED-ATOMIC save: usearch save() is NOT atomic temp+rename — write index+sidecar+manifest to temps, fsync, rename, with the MANIFEST rename as the single commit point (crash before it leaves the old trio intact). (3) HARDEN the root-relative key: strip_prefix failure must warn + use a canonical fallback, NOT the silent raw-path fallback that reverts to the original bug. CAVEATS to document (not blockers): load() is scale-bounded (revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo); i8 is a REAL option (exact_search_i8 + separate quantized index + quantized query) deferred to ccg, not irrelevant. Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).
2026-06-02 ROUND-3 (Codex re-review, design doc 9d5d8d5): crash-safe save REVISED — NOT 'rename index+sidecar then manifest last' (that clobbers the live pair); use GENERATION-SUFFIXED immutable files (index/meta/manifest.<gen>) + a CURRENT pointer whose atomic rename is the SOLE commit point + dir fsync + keep gen-1 as rollback (doc §7.1). Manifest must cover ALL vector/boundary inputs: + model_revision (tokenizer+weights), chunk_params, walker_version, search_mode, keys_checksum (sorted vector-key-set digest, catches index/sidecar drift) — not just model name. Per-file record adds mtime+size (reconcile signal). l5d MUST keep a content-addressed (content_hash,model,embed_schema)->vector dedup layer SEPARATE from key->vector, else renames/ordinal-churn become real re-embeds. Snippets = lazy source read at query time (no sidecar bloat). Load() rejects any manifest mismatch -> full rebuild.
2026-06-02 VERIFIED on current checkout by command: TLDR_QUIET=1 TLDR_EVAL_STORE=1 cargo run -p tldr-core --release --example semantic_eval -- crates/tldr-core/src. Dense baseline: R@5=0.750 (39/52), R@10=0.827 (43/52), MRR=0.599. Store equivalence: order-identical=39, TIE(gap<1e-6)=12, EPSILON(<1e-4)=1, REAL(>=1e-4)=0, equivalent modulo ties/epsilon=52/52. This satisfies l5d results-equivalence: no genuine ranking divergence; only arbitrary equal-score/epsilon boundary ordering.
close_reason: Accepted after local n=52 eval verification: usearch VectorStore exact-search path has REAL divergences=0 and is equivalent modulo ties/epsilon (52/52); dense baseline captured; crash-safe persistence/build/sidecar steps already committed.

title: Define MmapVectorStore format + abstraction
description: Design the on-disk format and the Rust abstraction for the vector store. Format: header (magic, version, model name, dim, count) + contiguous f32 vectors region (fixed stride = dim*4, 4-byte aligned) + a hash->offset index map (bincode) at the tail. One file per embedding model (dim is fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / arctic-m 768 / arctic-l 1024). Key = 64-bit hash(content_hash + file_path + function_name) -- same identity as today's CacheKey minus model (model implied by file). Define a trait so the backend is swappable. Must support zero-copy &[f32] reads from the mmap.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). usearch defines the on-disk index format + abstraction; no custom MmapVectorStore format to design. Residual (thin wrapper + metadata sidecar) folded into reframed TLDR-l5d.

title: Lock backend + GC decisions for mmap store
description: Finalize the two open design forks before implementation: (1) Backend = plain mmap (memmap2 + bincode hash->offset map) vs usearch. Leaning plain mmap: usearch builds HNSW on every add() (adds cost to the embed path, our 2nd bottleneck) for a search we don't use; pure-Rust avoids a C++ build dep; brute-force stays exact at any scale this tool targets. (2) GC = reachability-on-full-reindex (leaning; drops cached_at/file_mtime, entry becomes key->Vec<f32>) vs timestamp bookkeeping file. Output: a recorded decision (bd decision) that unblocks the impl children.
design: DECIDED: usearch. C++ build dep (cxx bridge) accepted by user 2026-06-01. usearch exact_search_f32 + view (mmap) + save/load + ScalarKind cover the exact-search + load + persistence + quant goals as a maintained library. GC fork: GC-by-reachability on full re-index (usearch index rebuilt from chunks; drops per-entry timestamps -> simplest, matches the rebuild model). Cascade recorded in memory 'usearch-discovery'/'decision-locked'.
close_reason: DECIDED: usearch (C++ dep accepted). The mmap-vs-usearch fork is resolved in favor of usearch — exact_search_f32 + view + save/load + ScalarKind. GC = reachability-on-full-reindex. Unblocks the (reframed) impl children.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DECISION EVIDENCE (2026-06-01): The mmap-vs-usearch fork was framed on a FALSE premise. This issue's description says usearch 'builds HNSW on every add() for a search we dont use' and implies approximate-only. Scanned usearch/rust/lib.rs: it has exact_search_f32 (L456) = EXACT brute-force, and view (L542) = mmap load. So usearch delivers ac0's EXACT goal (mmap-backed exact search) as a maintained library, collapsing the 8 ac0 impl children into one integration. The HNSW-build-on-add cost is negligible vs the ~30min CPU embed. The decision therefore reduces to a SINGLE axis: usearch adds a C++ compiler dependency (cxx bridge) to the build/CI; plain mmap stays pure-Rust. RECOMMENDATION: usearch, unless 'no C++ in build' is a hard ship constraint. Awaiting that one confirmation before recording the bd decision + superseding 7kf/8pt/k4q.

title: Replace JSON embedding cache with usearch-backed vector store (keep exact search)
description:
## EPIC: Replace the JSON embedding cache with a usearch vector store

### The measured problem
Cold `tldr semantic/similar/embed` = ~4,300ms JSON parse + 500ms model load + 200ms query embed + ~0.1ms brute-force cosine. The 1.4GB cache.json (text-encoded float vectors, multiple models) is loaded IN FULL to serve a handful of lookups. The brute-force cosine is NOT the bottleneck; the JSON load is.

### Decisions — LOCKED (2026-06-01/02, incl. Codex review)
- Backend = **usearch** (NOT hand-rolled memmap2+bincode; the old "usearch vs plain mmap" open decision is CLOSED).
- **exact_search_f32** (NOT HNSW — exact/100% recall, vendor-recommended <1M vectors).
- **load() into a WRITABLE RAM index**, NOT view()/mmap (view() is read-only #97; at ~58MB f32 load() is cheap and keeps the index writable, which incremental needs). load() is scale-bounded — revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo.
- **f32 first** (i8 = TLDR-ccg; separate quantized index + quantized query).
- Metric = cos (unit-normalized vectors).
- Win: 1.4GB JSON -> ~58MB f32 binary; daemon RSS ~2.3GB -> ~0.5GB.

### Sidecar — SCOPE EVOLVED (resolved with Codex review)
Original cold-CLI scope: ALL metadata was rebuilt by chunk_code() at search time, so NO persistent sidecar was needed (verified: cache.rs stored only {vector, cached_at, file_mtime}). That no longer holds. With the warm DAEMON path now shipped (TLDR-atc), the store is PERSISTED and served from a resident daemon, so a restart must serve results WITHOUT re-chunking the whole tree. Therefore a metadata **SIDECAR + MANIFEST** is now a **correctness** requirement of l5d (not optional, not deferrable to t8f). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (§4 sidecar/manifest, §7 crash-safe save).

### Targets / honesty
- Cold first-load: ~14s (1.4GB JSON) -> sub-second (usearch load()).
- Warm daemon repeat query: ~45ms — ALREADY ACHIEVED via atc + the root-relative cache-key fix.
- Do NOT promise a fixed latency for directory searches; the sidecar now persists metadata so warm dir searches can skip re-chunking (measure before promising).

### PASS/FAIL = results-equivalence, not latency
Exact brute-force + f32 kept -> new ranked results must be IDENTICAL to the JSON-cache path (n=52 eval + captured baselines). Latency improvement is necessary but NOT sufficient.

### Children
l5d (usearch store + sidecar + manifest + crash-safe save), m01 (rewire EmbeddingCache onto the store, keep a content-addressed dedup layer), ccg (i8 compact mode), t8f (incremental delta), zr6 (filtered/scoped search).
design:
RESOLVED design (supersedes the earlier 'plain mmap vs usearch' + 'NO sidecar' framing).

## Bottleneck (measured)
Cold query = 4.3s JSON parse + 0.5s model load + 0.2s query embed + ~0.1ms cosine. The brute-force cosine is irrelevant; HNSW would make it 0.5ms AND approximate. KEEP exact brute-force semantics via usearch exact_search_f32. KEEP f32.

## Store (usearch)
- key (u64) -> vector. add_f32 on build; remove(key) for deltas; exact_search_f32 for query; save/load (writable RAM, not view()).
- One logical store per model (dim fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / m 768 / l 1024).
- Content-addressed DEDUP layer kept SEPARATE from key->vector: a (content_hash, model, embed_schema) -> vector lookup so renames/ordinal-churn re-add without re-embedding (the current EmbeddingCache role; do NOT drop it).

## Sidecar + manifest (l5d correctness — see design doc)
- Sidecar: key -> {identity, file_rel_path, fn, class, line range, content_hash}; per-file record {keys, mtime, size}.
- Manifest covers EVERY input that changes vectors or chunk boundaries: format_version, generation, embedding_model, model_revision (tokenizer+weights), dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, search_mode, embed_schema, chunk_params, walker_version, root, chunk_count, keys_checksum, sidecar_checksum. load() rejects any mismatch -> full rebuild.
- Crash-safe save: generation-suffixed immutable files + a CURRENT pointer whose atomic rename is the sole commit point + dir fsync + keep gen-1 (design doc §7.1). usearch save() is NOT atomic by itself.
- Snippets: read lazily from source at query time, validated by content_hash, degrade gracefully (no sidecar bloat).

## GC
content_hash in the dedup key => changed content orphans old entries. GC-by-reachability on full re-index (drop keys not re-added) OR a small key->timestamp file. (Decide in m01.)

## Verification = results-equivalence
Captured baseline outputs (scores+snippets) + the n=52 eval are the golden reference. Exact brute-force + f32 => IDENTICAL ranked results old vs new. Latency alone is not pass/fail.
notes:
## Baseline Measurements (JSON-based, release build)

Cache: ~/Library/Caches/tldr/embeddings/cache.json = 1.1 GB, 117,641 entries

### Completed Tests

| Test | Command | Wall time | Internal latency_ms | Result |
|------|---------|-----------|---------------------|--------|
| 1. Fresh embed (cached) | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,393ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 2. Cached embed | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,315ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 3. Semantic search (single file) | `tldr semantic "parse json config" config.rs` | 5.22s | 795ms | 10 results, cache_hit=false |
| 5. Cold search (single file) | `tldr semantic "error handling" config.rs` | 7.37s | 1,098ms | 10 results |
| 6. Config model check | `tldr embed config.rs` (config=arctic-l) | 4.41s | 4,370ms | model: arctic-l ✓ |
| 7. CLI flag override | `tldr embed config.rs --model arctic-s` | 4.41s | 4,378ms | model: arctic-s ✓ |
| 8. Existing tests | `cargo test semantic` | 60s (compile) / 6.88s (run) | — | 8 passed, 17 ignored |

### Key observation
Even for a CACHED 28-chunk file, embed takes 4.3s wall time. That's ~4.3s spent loading/parsing the 1.1GB JSON cache, NOT embedding. The actual search latency (795ms-1098ms) is on top of the cache parse.

### Pending Tests (running — first-time arctic-l embed of 14K chunks)
| Test | Command | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| 4. Similar function search | `tldr similar config.rs --function from_path` | Embedding 14,144 chunks (first time arctic-l) |
| 5b. Cold search (full src/) | `tldr semantic "error handling" src/` | Embedding full directory (first time arctic-l) |

These will complete and be recorded. The one-time embed cost is NOT part of the comparison — only the subsequent cached searches matter for the usearch benchmark.
2026-06-02 DECISION RESOLVED + reconciled with Codex review (thread a708b3161b2e63d89). (1) The 'usearch vs plain mmap (memmap2+bincode)' open decision is CLOSED -> usearch library (decision-locked memory: decision-locked-2026-06-01-vector-store-usearch). The hand-rolled memmap2 plan (and TLDR-j8c 'MmapVectorStore') is OBSOLETE. (2) Backend: usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index (NOT view()/mmap) — view() is read-only and at ~58MB f32 mmap earns nothing; load() keeps the index writable for deltas. load() is scale-bounded: revisit view() for the read path past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo. (3) SIDECAR REVISED: this epic previously said 'NO sidecar needed (metadata from re-chunk at search time)'. That holds for a COLD single-shot CLI, but NOT once the store is PERSISTED + served from a resident daemon (TLDR-atc, now done): a restart would have to re-chunk the whole tree to recover result metadata. So a metadata SIDECAR + MANIFEST is now a CORRECTNESS requirement of l5d, not optional. KEEP these still-valid caveats from the original epic text: exact brute-force (not HNSW); f32 first (i8=ccg, separate quantized index); single-file vs directory latency differ — do NOT promise 730ms for directory searches (re-chunk cost stays unless the sidecar persists metadata — which it now does). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).
